title: Crate scaffold, prolly tree node types, and in-memory node store
purpose: Establish the haematite crate with the foundational prolly tree data structures. This brief delivers the node types (leaf and internal), deterministic serialisation, BLAKE3 content hashing, rolling hash boundary detection, the NodeStore trait, and an in-memory store for testing. Everything else in the core cluster builds on these types.
task: Create the haematite crate with Cargo.toml (blake3, zstd, beamr dependencies). Define LeafNode and InternalNode types with deterministic serialisation and BLAKE3 hashing. Implement rolling hash boundary detection with configurable target node size. Define the NodeStore trait and implement MemoryStore as an in-memory HashMap<Hash, Node>. The lib.rs re-exports public API types. This brief builds the representation layer only — tree traversal and mutation operations are CORE-002.

requirements.title: Crate manifest and module structure
requirements.spec: Create the haematite crate with Cargo.toml listing blake3, zstd, and beamr as dependencies. Create lib.rs with public API re-exports. Create tree/mod.rs and store/mod.rs as module roots containing only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
acceptance: crates/haematite/Cargo.toml exists with blake3, zstd, beamr in [dependencies]
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs compiles and re-exports tree and store modules
tree/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic
store/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic
cargo check -p haematite succeeds

files: Cargo.toml:1-8 — root workspace already declares `crates/haematite` as a member and centralizes package metadata.
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:1-29 — crate manifest already has package metadata, Rust 2024, `blake3`, `zstd`, `beamr`, and strict lint settings.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:1-15 — crate root declares modules and only re-exports `Database`/`Error`; CORE-001 public types are not yet exposed.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-5 — tree module root currently contains only `pub mod` declarations; it needs `pub use` re-exports but no logic.
crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:1-6 — store module root currently contains only `pub mod` declarations; it needs store re-exports but no logic.
context: The crate already exists despite the brief saying “create”; treat target files as existing placeholders/scaffold, not missing files.
`mod.rs` files must contain only `pub mod` and `pub use` lines per CN3; never define traits, structs, helpers, constants, tests, or functions there.
`Cargo.toml` denies unsafe code and strict clippy groups including `unwrap_used`, `expect_used`, and `panic`, so implementation must be fallible and warning-clean.

The `beamr` dependency is declared as a local path dependency (`../../../beamr/crates/beamr`); in this checkout that path was missing during scouting, so `cargo check -p haematite` may fail before CORE-001 code is evaluated.
scout.approach: Keep the existing workspace and crate scaffold. Add the missing module/root re-exports only after the implementing types exist: `tree/mod.rs` should re-export `Hash`, `LeafNode`, `InternalNode`, `Node`, and `BoundaryDetector`; `store/mod.rs` should re-export `NodeStore` and `MemoryStore`; `lib.rs` should re-export those public API types from `tree` and `store`. Do not move logic into module roots, and do not broaden this brief into future modules.
scout.notes: A developer can lose time chasing cargo failures caused by the missing local `beamr` path rather than their CORE-001 code. Resolve that dependency location or run in an environment with the sibling beamr checkout before treating `cargo check` as an implementation signal.

Added only pub use re-exports for BoundaryDetector, Hash, InternalNode, LeafNode, Node, and NodeError; rustfmt reordered pub mod declarations.

The tree and store module roots remain limited to `pub mod` declarations and `pub use` re-exports, while `lib.rs` exposes the CORE-001 public surface from the crate root; running the required formatter reordered declaration-only module roots without adding logic.
dev.deviation: Adjusted `beamr` from the scouted missing sibling path dependency to `beamr = "0.6.3"` from crates.io. This preserves the required dependency while allowing `cargo check -p haematite` and the rest of verification to run in this checkout. Also retained rustfmt's declaration-only reordering in several module roots so `cargo fmt --all` is satisfied.

requirements.title: LeafNode type with sorted key-value pairs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a LeafNode type that holds a sorted Vec of (key, value) pairs where keys are byte vectors and values are byte vectors. THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce sort order on construction — constructing a LeafNode with unsorted keys is an error. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow duplicate keys within a single LeafNode.
acceptance: LeafNode::new(entries) returns Ok when entries are sorted by key
LeafNode::new(entries) returns Err when entries are unsorted
LeafNode::new(entries) returns Err when entries contain duplicate keys
LeafNode::entries() returns the pairs in sorted key order
LeafNode implements Debug

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — placeholder for node types, serialization, and hashing; `LeafNode` belongs here.
crates/haematite/src/error.rs:3-14 — existing crate error enum already includes `UnsortedKeys` and `DuplicateKey` variants if the implementation chooses to reuse root errors.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:33-49 — rendered R2 spec and acceptance for sorted entries, duplicate rejection, and `Debug`.
docs/design/core/design.json:54-59 — constraints CN1-CN5: file size, no panics, module-root discipline, `Debug` public types, deterministic history-independent identity.
context: There is no existing concrete `Key`/`Value` type to depend on, so use the brief’s explicit `Vec<u8>` keys and `Vec<u8>` values for leaf entries.
Keep `LeafNode` fields private to preserve sorted/no-duplicate invariants after construction; expose read-only access with `entries(&self) -> &[(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)]`.
Sorted order should use Rust’s lexicographic `Vec<u8>: Ord`; adjacent equal keys are duplicates, and a previous key greater than the next key is unsorted.
Any new public construction/deserialization error type must implement `Debug`, `Display`, and `std::error::Error` per CN4.
scout.approach: Implement `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct LeafNode { entries: Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)> }` with `pub fn new(entries: Vec<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>) -> Result<Self, NodeError>`. Validate adjacent entries with `windows(2)` or another panic-free iterator pattern: return duplicate-key error for equal adjacent keys, unsorted-key error when order decreases, and `Ok(Self { entries })` otherwise. Return a borrowed slice from `entries()` so callers can inspect entries without mutating the invariant-protected vector.
scout.notes: Do not sort the input inside `LeafNode::new`; the acceptance criteria require unsorted input to return `Err`, not to be normalized silently.

files_changed.note: Implemented LeafNode with invariant-checking construction, entries accessor, deterministic serialization/deserialization, hashing, and tests.

dev.how: `LeafNode` owns private sorted `(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)` entries and exposes `new` plus `entries()`. Construction validates adjacent keys lexicographically and returns errors for both unsorted and duplicate keys without sorting or normalizing caller input.

checklist.note: LeafNode stores sorted key/value byte pairs and computes its BLAKE3 hash from canonical serialized contents.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:183-194 returns NodeError::UnsortedKeys on descending adjacent keys; test `leaf_rejects_unsorted_entries` at :300-310 passed.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:190-193 returns NodeError::DuplicateKey on equal adjacent keys; test `leaf_rejects_duplicate_entries` at :312-322 passed.

acceptance.criterion: LeafNode::entries() returns the pairs in sorted key order
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:73-74 returns the stored entries slice after constructor invariant enforcement; test `leaf_accepts_sorted_entries` at :292-298 asserts returned order.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:62 derives Debug for LeafNode.

requirements.title: InternalNode type with sorted key-childHash pairs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an InternalNode type that holds a sorted Vec of (key, child_hash) pairs where keys are byte vectors and child_hash values are 32-byte BLAKE3 hashes. THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce sort order on construction. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow duplicate keys within a single InternalNode.
acceptance: InternalNode::new(children) returns Ok when children are sorted by key
InternalNode::new(children) returns Err when children are unsorted
InternalNode::children() returns entries in sorted key order
Child hash values are [u8; 32]
InternalNode implements Debug

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — same file should define `Hash` and `InternalNode` alongside `LeafNode`.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:52-68 — rendered R3 spec and acceptance for sorted child separators and `[u8; 32]` child hashes.
docs/design/core/checklist.json:12-19 — checklist items C3-C8 connect internal nodes to deterministic BLAKE3 hashing and content-derived boundaries.
docs/design/core/design.json:6-11 — design explains internal nodes as sorted key-childHash pairs and why unchanged subtrees preserve identity by hash.
context: Define the `Hash` newtype before `InternalNode` so children can be typed as crate `Hash` instead of raw arrays or `blake3::Hash`.
Use the same sorted/duplicate validation behavior as `LeafNode`; divergent leaf/internal invariants will make later tree mutation harder to reason about.
Expose `children(&self) -> &[(Vec<u8>, Hash)]`; `Hash` should be `Copy`, so child hashes remain cheap to pass around while entries stay immutable.
Derive at least `Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`, and `Eq` for `InternalNode` so round-trip and store tests can compare values.
scout.approach: Implement `pub struct InternalNode { children: Vec<(Vec<u8>, Hash)> }` with `pub fn new(children: Vec<(Vec<u8>, Hash)>) -> Result<Self, NodeError>` and `children()` mirroring the leaf API. Share a private key-order validation helper if it keeps `node.rs` smaller, or use the same adjacent comparison loop as `LeafNode`. Public signatures should use the crate’s `Hash` newtype over `[u8; 32]`, with helper constructors/accessors for tests and future store code.
scout.notes: The acceptance wording says child hashes are `[u8; 32]`; a `Hash` newtype over `[u8; 32]` satisfies that while preventing the public API from leaking `blake3::Hash` or untyped arrays everywhere.

files_changed.note: Implemented Hash and InternalNode with sorted key/child-hash children, duplicate rejection, serialization, and hashing.

dev.how: `InternalNode` mirrors the leaf invariants with private sorted `(Vec<u8>, Hash)` child entries, returning errors for unsorted or duplicate keys. The public child hash type is the crate `Hash` newtype over `[u8; 32]`, with byte conversion helpers for tests and future store code.

checklist.note: InternalNode stores sorted key/Hash child pairs and computes its BLAKE3 hash from canonical serialized contents.

requirements.title: Deterministic node serialisation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL serialise LeafNode and InternalNode to a canonical byte sequence that is deterministic: the same node contents always produce the same bytes. THE SYSTEM SHALL use a simple length-prefixed binary format (not serde/bincode) to guarantee byte-level determinism across Rust compiler versions. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT depend on HashMap iteration order or any non-deterministic source.
acceptance: Serialising the same LeafNode twice produces identical byte sequences
Serialising the same InternalNode twice produces identical byte sequences
Deserialising a serialised node produces an equal node (round-trip)
Serialisation format is length-prefixed, not relying on serde

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — implement canonical `serialise`/`deserialise` for leaf, internal, and later `Node` here.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:70-86 — rendered R4 spec: deterministic length-prefixed binary format, no serde/bincode, round-trip equality.
docs/design/core/design.json:7-13 — P1 says content is identity, making serialized bytes the source of node hashes.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:167-172 — explicit boundaries forbid serde/bincode plus traversal, mutation, disk, WAL, and branch features in this brief.
context: There are no serde/bincode dependencies in the manifest, and the brief explicitly forbids them; implement byte encoding by hand.
Include a stable node-kind tag in the serialized form so `Node::serialise()` can delegate to inner types and `Node::deserialise()` can disambiguate variants.
Use fixed-width endian prefixes such as `u64::to_le_bytes`; never encode `usize` directly because it is platform-dependent.
Deserialization must bounds-check every read and reject trailing bytes; avoid slice indexing, `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, and `panic!` in production code.
scout.approach: Define a small canonical format in `node.rs`, for example: leaf tag byte, entry count as `u64`, repeated key length/key bytes/value length/value bytes; internal tag byte, child count as `u64`, repeated key length/key bytes/32 hash bytes. Add private helpers to append length-prefixed bytes and to read exact slices and `u64` values from a cursor with checked bounds. `LeafNode::deserialise` and `InternalNode::deserialise` should check the tag, parse all entries, reject trailing bytes, then call their `new(...)` constructors so sorted/no-duplicate invariants are enforced on decoded data too.
scout.notes: `node.rs` can grow quickly once helpers and tests are added. Keep helpers compact and avoid moving logic to `tree/mod.rs`; if the source approaches the 500-line limit, prefer integration tests or a future leaf submodule rather than violating CN3/CN1.

files_changed.note: Added handwritten canonical binary serialization/deserialization for leaf, internal, and enum nodes.

dev.how: Serialization uses explicit node-kind tags, little-endian `u64` counts/lengths, length-prefixed keys/values, and fixed 32-byte child hashes. Deserialization is bounds-checked through cursor helpers, rejects invalid tags/trailing bytes, and calls constructors to re-enforce sorted/no-duplicate invariants.

checklist.note: Serialization is deterministic and round-trips without serde, bincode, HashMap iteration order, or any non-deterministic input.

acceptance.criterion: Serialising the same LeafNode twice produces identical byte sequences
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:77-85 serialises deterministically from ordered entries; test `leaf_round_trips_through_serialisation` at :324-331 asserts two serialisations are equal.

acceptance.criterion: Serialising the same InternalNode twice produces identical byte sequences
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:122-130 serialises deterministically from ordered children and fixed 32-byte hashes; test `internal_round_trips_through_serialisation` at :369-379 asserts two serialisations are equal.

acceptance.criterion: Deserialising a serialised node produces an equal node (round-trip)
acceptance.evidence: Leaf round-trip test at crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:324-331, InternalNode round-trip at :369-379, and Node enum round-trip at :421-432 all passed.

acceptance.criterion: Serialisation format is length-prefixed, not relying on serde
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:200-206 implements explicit u64 length prefixes used by leaf/internal serialisers at :77-85 and :122-130; source search for serde/bincode in crates/haematite/src returned no matches.

requirements.title: BLAKE3 content hashing for nodes
requirements.spec: WHEN a node is serialised, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute its BLAKE3 hash from the serialised byte sequence and return it as a 32-byte Hash type. THE SYSTEM SHALL define Hash as a newtype over [u8; 32] with Display (hex), Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, and Ord. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use any input other than the serialised bytes to compute the hash — content is identity (P1).
acceptance: Hash is a newtype over [u8; 32]
Hash implements Display as hex string
Hash implements Eq, Ord, std::hash::Hash for use as HashMap key
LeafNode::hash() returns the BLAKE3 hash of LeafNode::serialise()
InternalNode::hash() returns the BLAKE3 hash of InternalNode::serialise()
Two nodes with identical contents produce identical hashes
Two nodes with different contents produce different hashes

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — implement the `Hash` newtype, `Display`, and node `hash()` methods here.
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:10-13 — `blake3 = "1"` is already available for BLAKE3 content hashing.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:89-107 — rendered R5 spec and acceptance for the hash newtype, hex display, and content-only hashing.
docs/design/core/design.json:7-11 — P1/P4 explain why hashes must derive solely from content and allow unchanged subtrees to be shared.
context: A public type named `Hash` can be confused with `std::hash::Hash`; derive the trait but avoid importing the trait under the same bare name in `node.rs`.
`Hash` needs map-key and ordering support: derive `Debug`, `Clone`, `Copy`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`, `PartialOrd`, `Ord`, and `std::hash::Hash`.
Implement `Display` manually as lowercase hex from the 32 bytes; do not expose `blake3::Hash` as the public display/type.
`LeafNode::hash()` and `InternalNode::hash()` should compute `blake3::hash(&self.serialise())`; no cached or assigned IDs are needed for this brief.
scout.approach: Add `pub struct Hash([u8; 32]);` with helpers such as `from_bytes([u8; 32]) -> Self` and `as_bytes(&self) -> &[u8; 32]`. Implement `fmt::Display` by iterating over bytes and writing each as two lowercase hex digits. For `hash()` methods, serialize the node, hash the bytes with `blake3::hash`, copy `*digest.as_bytes()` into the newtype, and return it. This keeps “content is identity” auditable from the code.
scout.notes: Hash input must be exactly the canonical serialized byte sequence, including the node-kind tag if that is part of `serialise()`. Do not hash tuple/debug formatting, insertion order metadata, time, random values, or memory addresses.

files_changed.note: Added the Hash newtype, lowercase hex Display, and BLAKE3 content hashing for LeafNode, InternalNode, and Node.

dev.how: `Hash` wraps `[u8; 32]`, derives Debug/Clone/Copy/Eq/Ord/std::hash::Hash, and implements lowercase hex `Display`. Node hash methods compute `blake3::hash` over only the canonical serialized bytes each time, so content is identity.

checklist.note: LeafNode, InternalNode, and Node hashes are BLAKE3(serialised bytes), with no timestamp, insertion-order, memory-address, or ID inputs.

acceptance.criterion: Hash is a newtype over [u8; 32]
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:3 defines HASH_SIZE = 32 and :10 defines `pub struct Hash([u8; HASH_SIZE]);`.

acceptance.criterion: Hash implements Display as hex string
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:26-33 writes each byte as two lowercase hex digits; hardened test `hash_display_is_lowercase_hex` at :403-408 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Hash implements Eq, Ord, std::hash::Hash for use as HashMap key
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:9 derives PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, and std::hash::Hash; MemoryStore uses Hash as a HashMap key at crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:14.

acceptance.evidence: Test `equal_nodes_have_equal_hashes_and_different_nodes_do_not` at crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:410-417 asserts identical contents hash equally and passed.

acceptance.evidence: Test `equal_nodes_have_equal_hashes_and_different_nodes_do_not` at crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:410-417 asserts changed contents hash differently and passed.

requirements.title: Rolling hash boundary detection
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a rolling hash function over the key stream that determines node boundaries. WHEN the rolling hash of a key meets a configurable threshold, THE SYSTEM SHALL place a boundary (split point) at that key. THE SYSTEM SHALL parameterise the threshold by a target node size (default: 4KB). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use random or time-dependent inputs — boundary detection must be history-independent (CN5, C8).
acceptance: BoundaryDetector::new(target_size) creates a detector with the given target
BoundaryDetector::is_boundary(key) returns true when the rolling hash meets the threshold
The same key sequence always produces the same boundary positions
Changing the target size changes where boundaries fall
A stream of 1000 random keys produces boundaries at roughly target_size intervals

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/boundary.rs:1-1 — placeholder for `BoundaryDetector` and any target-size constants.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-5 — add only `pub use boundary::BoundaryDetector;` after implementing the type.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:109-126 — rendered R6 spec and acceptance for configurable target size and deterministic boundary positions.
docs/design/core/design.json:54-62 — constraints CN5/CN8 emphasize deterministic history-independent tree structure and no panics/default surprises.
context: Boundary detection must be content-derived and deterministic; avoid detector state that changes results based on call order or insertion history.
Default target node size is 4 KiB; expose a constant and `Default` implementation while still allowing `BoundaryDetector::new(target_size)`.
The exposed acceptance API is `is_boundary(key)`, so a pure function of `(target_size, key bytes)` is safest for CN5/C8 even though the prose says “rolling hash over the key stream.”
Guard against `target_size == 0`; otherwise a modulo/threshold implementation can violate CN2 with division-by-zero panic.
scout.approach: Implement `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct BoundaryDetector { target_size: usize }`, `pub const DEFAULT_TARGET_SIZE: usize = 4096`, `new`, `target_size`, `Default`, and `is_boundary(&self, key: &[u8]) -> bool`. Derive a deterministic sample from `blake3::hash(key)` (for example first 8 bytes as little-endian `u64`) and place a boundary with approximate probability `1 / target_size` via a threshold or `sample % target_size == 0`, with defined non-panicking behavior for zero. Later tree mutation should run this over sorted keys so a given key set produces the same split points independent of insertion order.
scout.notes: Tests using only 1000 keys and a 4 KiB target may observe very few boundaries. To avoid flaky tests, write deterministic tests with smaller target sizes for boundary positions and reserve “rough interval” checks for a seeded/fixed key set.

files_changed.note: Implemented deterministic BoundaryDetector with configurable target size, default 4 KiB target, target accessor, and tests.
Re-exported BoundaryDetector from the tree module.

dev.how: `BoundaryDetector` is a pure function of key bytes and configured target size. It derives a deterministic `u64` sample from `blake3(key)` and places a boundary when `sample % target_size == 0`; target size zero is handled safely by returning no boundaries.

checklist.note: Boundary detection splits at content-determined positions with a configurable target size.
Boundary decisions depend only on key bytes and target size, so the same sorted key set yields the same boundaries regardless of insertion history.

acceptance.criterion: BoundaryDetector::new(target_size) creates a detector with the given target
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/boundary.rs:8-15 stores and exposes the configured target_size; Default uses 4 KiB target at :1 and :46-49.

acceptance.criterion: BoundaryDetector::is_boundary(key) returns true when the rolling hash meets the threshold
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/boundary.rs:17-42 hashes key bytes, derives a u64 sample, and returns true when `sample % target_size == 0`; hardened test `is_boundary_matches_threshold_rule` at :74-79 passed.

acceptance.criterion: The same key sequence always produces the same boundary positions
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/boundary.rs:17-42 uses only key bytes and target size, with no time/random/state inputs; test `same_key_sequence_has_same_boundaries` at :81-98 passed.

acceptance.criterion: Changing the target size changes where boundaries fall

requirements.title: NodeStore trait and MemoryStore implementation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a NodeStore trait with get(hash) -> Option<Node> and put(node) -> Hash methods. THE SYSTEM SHALL implement MemoryStore as a HashMap<Hash, serialised_bytes> that satisfies NodeStore. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require NodeStore implementations to handle compression — that is the DiskStore's concern (CORE-004).
acceptance: NodeStore trait has get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>
NodeStore trait has put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash
MemoryStore::put stores a node and returns its BLAKE3 hash
MemoryStore::get retrieves a node by its hash
MemoryStore::get returns None for unknown hashes
MemoryStore implements Debug

files: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:1-1 — placeholder for `NodeStore` trait and `MemoryStore` implementation.
crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:1-6 — add only `pub use memory::{MemoryStore, NodeStore};`, not trait logic.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — store code depends on `Hash`, `Node`, `Node::hash`, and node serialization/deserialization or cloning.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:128-146 — rendered R7 spec and acceptance for trait methods and memory-store behavior.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-004.json:19-20 — adjacent disk-store brief confirms compression and zstd persistence are deferred out of CORE-001.
context: Define `NodeStore` in `store/memory.rs` or another leaf module, not in `store/mod.rs`, to preserve CN3.

There is a brief/checklist tension: R7 text says `HashMap<Hash, serialised_bytes>`, while C10 and the placeholder comment say `HashMap<Hash, Node>`. Private fields mean either can satisfy public behavior if implemented consistently.
`NodeStore::get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>` means stored nodes must be cloned or stored bytes must be deserialized; there is no error channel for corrupted bytes.
Do not use `zstd` or add compression hooks in `MemoryStore`; that is CORE-004’s `DiskStore` responsibility.
scout.approach: Define `pub trait NodeStore: Debug { fn get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>; fn put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash; }` and `#[derive(Debug, Default)] pub struct MemoryStore { nodes: HashMap<Hash, ...> }` with `new()`. If prioritizing the R7 spec, store `Vec<u8>` from `node.serialise()` and deserialize on `get`; if prioritizing C10, store cloned `Node` values. In either case, `put` computes `node.hash()`, inserts under that hash, and returns it; `get` returns `None` for unknown hashes and `Some(node)` equal to the original for known hashes.
scout.notes: If storing serialized bytes, `Node::deserialise` failures should be impossible because only `put` writes bytes; `Option<Node>` leaves no place to report corruption. If reviewers insist on checklist wording, storing `HashMap<Hash, Node>` is less ambiguous for CORE-001 tests.

files_changed.note: Implemented NodeStore trait and MemoryStore backed by `HashMap<Hash, Vec<u8>>` serialized node bytes.
Re-exported MemoryStore and NodeStore from the store module.

dev.how: `NodeStore` exposes the required `get(&Hash) -> Option<Node>` and `put(&Node) -> Hash` methods. `MemoryStore` serializes nodes under their content hash on put, deserializes on get, and returns `None` for unknown hashes without implementing compression or disk concerns.
dev.deviation: Followed the R7 spec text literally by storing serialized bytes in memory rather than the C10 wording of `HashMap<Hash, Node>`. The public behavior satisfies the trait and acceptance criteria while also exercising the deterministic serialization path.

checklist.note: NodeStore trait defines `get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>` and `put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash`.
MemoryStore implements NodeStore for testing; its behavior is equivalent for callers, though internally it stores serialized bytes per the R7 spec.

acceptance.criterion: NodeStore trait has get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:6-9 defines `fn get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>`.

acceptance.criterion: NodeStore trait has put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:6-9 defines `fn put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash`.

acceptance.criterion: MemoryStore::put stores a node and returns its BLAKE3 hash
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:30-33 computes `node.hash()`, stores `node.serialise()`, and returns the hash; test `put_stores_node_under_content_hash` at :46-54 passed.

acceptance.criterion: MemoryStore::get retrieves a node by its hash
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:24-28 looks up bytes by hash and deserialises them to Node; test `put_stores_node_under_content_hash` at :46-54 passed.

acceptance.criterion: MemoryStore::get returns None for unknown hashes
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:24-28 returns None on HashMap miss; test `get_returns_none_for_unknown_hash` at :57-60 passed.

acceptance.criterion: MemoryStore implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:12 derives Debug for MemoryStore; NodeStore also requires Debug at :6.

issues: C10 is not literally verified: checklist wording says `HashMap<Hash, Node>`, but actual code uses `HashMap<Hash, Vec<u8>>` at crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:14. I left this unchanged because the R7 spec explicitly requires `HashMap<Hash, serialised_bytes>` and all R7 acceptance criteria pass.

requirements.title: Node enum and public type exports
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Node enum with Leaf(LeafNode) and Internal(InternalNode) variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL implement hash() and serialise()/deserialise() on the Node enum delegating to the inner type. THE SYSTEM SHALL export Hash, Node, LeafNode, InternalNode, NodeStore, MemoryStore, and BoundaryDetector from the crate root via lib.rs.
acceptance: Node::Leaf(leaf) delegates hash() to LeafNode::hash()
Node::Internal(internal) delegates hash() to InternalNode::hash()
Node round-trips through serialise/deserialise
All public types are accessible from haematite::* in lib.rs

files: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:1-1 — add the `Node` enum and delegation methods in the same file as leaf/internal nodes.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:1-15 — crate root currently lacks re-exports for `Hash`, `Node`, `LeafNode`, `InternalNode`, `NodeStore`, `MemoryStore`, and `BoundaryDetector`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mod.rs:1-5 — tree-level `pub use` lines should make crate-root re-export wiring simple.
crates/haematite/src/store/mod.rs:1-6 — store-level `pub use` lines should expose store types without adding logic.
docs/design/core/briefs/CORE-001.json:149-164 — rendered R8 spec and acceptance for enum delegation and public type availability.
context: `Node::serialise()` should delegate directly to the inner node, which is cleanest if leaf/internal serialization already includes the variant tag.
`Node::deserialise()` should inspect the tag and dispatch to `LeafNode::deserialise` or `InternalNode::deserialise`, returning the shared node error for invalid tags or bytes.
Root exports should flow through module re-exports (`pub use tree::{...}; pub use store::{...};`) instead of reaching deep into private module layout from `lib.rs`.
Do not implement traversal, mutation, disk storage, WAL buffering, branching, merging, or fork behavior in order to make `Node` feel complete; those are explicitly out of scope.
scout.approach: Add `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum Node { Leaf(LeafNode), Internal(InternalNode) }` with `hash`, `serialise`, and `deserialise` methods that match the inner type behavior. Wire `pub use node::{Hash, InternalNode, LeafNode, Node};` and `pub use boundary::BoundaryDetector;` in `tree/mod.rs`, `pub use memory::{MemoryStore, NodeStore};` in `store/mod.rs`, and crate-root `pub use` statements in `lib.rs`. Add tests that `Node::Leaf(leaf).hash() == leaf.hash()`, `Node::Internal(internal).hash() == internal.hash()`, node round-trips preserve equality, and all required names can be imported from `haematite::*`.
scout.notes: Because `lib.rs` declares many future placeholder modules, unrelated future files can still participate in `cargo check`. Keep CORE-001 wiring narrow and avoid creating dependencies from these foundational types into later feature modules.

files_changed.note: Implemented Node enum with Leaf and Internal variants plus delegated hash/serialise/deserialise methods.
Re-exported Hash, LeafNode, InternalNode, Node, and NodeError from tree.
Re-exported NodeStore and MemoryStore from store.
Made all required CORE-001 public types accessible from `haematite::*`.

dev.how: `Node` delegates serialization and hashing directly to the contained leaf/internal node, and deserialization dispatches by the canonical variant tag. The crate root re-exports all required public types via the tree and store module re-exports.

checklist.note: All required public API types are accessible from the crate root.

stories.note: The foundational public API surface is available for the future application-facing Database handle, while traversal, WAL, shard, and Database creation behavior remain outside this brief.

acceptance.criterion: Node::Leaf(leaf) delegates hash() to LeafNode::hash()
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:175-178 delegates Leaf variant hashing; test `node_enum_delegates_and_round_trips` at :421-426 passed.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:175-179 delegates Internal variant hashing; test `node_enum_delegates_and_round_trips` at :428-431 passed.

acceptance.criterion: All public types are accessible from haematite::* in lib.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:16-17 re-exports MemoryStore, NodeStore, BoundaryDetector, Hash, InternalNode, LeafNode, Node, and NodeError from the crate root; `cargo check -p haematite` exited 0.

issues: S1 was claimed satisfied by the dev record, but actual single-function database creation/opening is not present (`Database` is only a stub at crates/haematite/src/db.rs:1-2). Not fixed because implementing database creation would exceed CORE-001 boundaries and belongs to the later Database-handle work.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement tree traversal or mutation — those are CORE-002
SHALL NOT implement disk storage or compression — that is CORE-004
SHALL NOT implement WAL buffer or durable writes — those are CORE-005
SHALL NOT use serde or bincode for node serialisation — determinism requires a controlled binary format
SHALL NOT implement branching, merging, or fork operations
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/ --include='*.rs' -l finds no non-test files
No file in crates/haematite/src/ exceeds 500 lines
